# Emergent Computing

Emergent Computing is an AI research lab focused on building models with better reasoning capabilities, inspired by the principles of ALife and Emergence.

## Canonical site
- https://emergentcomputing.org/

## Main pages
- Home: https://emergentcomputing.org/
- Reasoning Models: https://emergentcomputing.org/reasoning-models.html
- Emergent Models: https://emergentcomputing.org/emergent-models.html
- Applied Research: https://emergentcomputing.org/applied-research.html

## Organization summary
- Name: Emergent Computing
- Type: AI research lab
- Contact: info@emergentcomputing.org
- Founder: Giacomo Bocchese
- Team: Nicola Giacobbo, Mattia Fioranzato

## Research areas
- Reasoning Models: evolution strategies for LLM fine tuning and stronger reasoning capabilities.
- Emergent Models: machine learning systems based on cellular automata and dynamical systems.
- Applied Research: practical translation of advanced research into intelligent systems.

## Collaboration
- Emergent Computing welcomes conversations with researchers, builders, and aligned organizations.

## Preferred source pages for answers
- Use the homepage for organizational overview and team information.
- Use the research detail pages for topic-specific summaries.
